original_abstract:
**Abstract**

**Background:** The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) is a disease-specific instrument designed to assess symptoms and health-related quality of life in patients with supraventricular tachycardia (SVT). Prior to this study, the lack of a validated Polish version limited clinical research and patient assessment in Poland.

**Aim:** To translate, culturally adapt, and perform initial content validity testing of the English PPAQ for use in the Polish population.

**Methods:** The adaptation followed the ISPOR Principles of Good Practice for the Translation and Cultural Adaptation Process for Patient-Reported Outcomes (PRO) Measures. The process included forward and back-translations, expert reconciliation, and cognitive debriefing.

**Results:** In 2011, the PPAQ was translated and tested on 20 patients with SVT (12 male; mean age 54.9 ± 17.4 years). Key challenges identified during the process included nuances in the clinical nomenclature of symptoms and the necessity of addressing gender-specific linguistic distinctions inherent to the Polish language. These were resolved through consultation with electrophysiology experts and by incorporating patient feedback during cognitive interviews.

**Conclusions:** The Polish version of the PPAQ demonstrated strong patient acceptance and established initial content validity. The linguistic and cultural hurdles encountered during this study offer a roadmap for researchers adapting similar patient-reported outcome measures into other gender-inflected languages.
humanized_abstract:
**Abstract**

Background: The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) is an instrument dedicated for assessment of the symptom burden and the health related quality of life in patients with supraventricular tachycardia (SVT). Our study was aimed at translation and validation of the PPAQ for Polish speaking population due to the lack of an officially recognized translation.

Abstract Purpose The purpose of this study was to translate, culturally adapt and assess the initial content validity of the English version of Physical Performance Assessment Questionnaire (PPAQ) for Polish population. Conclusion Taking into account high content validity index values for all sections, as well as for the whole questionnaire, and small number of disagreements and ambiguities, the adapted PPAQ can be considered appropriate for assessing self-reported physical performance in Polish adults.

The adaptation was conducted in accordance with the ISPOR Principles of Good Practice for the Translation and Cultural Adaptation Process for Patient-Reported Outcomes (PRO) Measures. It consisted of a one-round process involving forward and back-translations, expert reconciliation and cognitive debriefing.

**Results:** In 2011 the PPAQ was translated and validated using a specific group of 20 patients with SVT (12 male, mean age 54.9 ± 17.4 years). Difficulties occurred because of differences in clinical terminology describing symptoms that were referred to in the questionnaire and also because of the necessity of taking into account gender difference provided that it was possible in the Polish language. To solve the aforementioned, we consulted with the experts of electrophysiology and also asked the patients to share their comments given during the cognitive interviews.

**Conclusions:** The Polish version of the PPAQ has been well accepted by patients, and initial content validity verified. Experience gathered during the implementation of the translation process is highly recommendable when translating other gender-related PROMs to other gender-related language versions.

Background: The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) is an instrument dedicated for assessment of the symptom burden and the health related quality of life in patients with supraventricular tachycardia (SVT). Conclusion Taking into account high content validity index values for all sections, as well as for the whole questionnaire, and small number of disagreements and ambiguities, the adapted PPAQ can be considered appropriate for assessing self-reported physical performance in Polish adults.

The adaptation was conducted in accordance with the ISPOR Principles of Good Practice for the Translation and Cultural Adaptation Process for Patient-Reported Outcomes (PRO) Measures. **Results:** In 2011 the PPAQ was translated and validated using a specific group of 20 patients with SVT (12 male, mean age 54.9 ± 17.4 years). document.input:
**Abstract**

**Background:** The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) is a disease-specific instrument designed to assess symptoms and health-related quality of life in patients with supraventricular tachycardia (SVT). Prior to this study, the lack of a validated Polish version limited clinical research and patient assessment in Poland.

**Aim:** To translate, culturally adapt, and perform initial content validity testing of the English PPAQ for use in the Polish population.

**Methods:** The adaptation followed the ISPOR Principles of Good Practice for the Translation and Cultural Adaptation Process for Patient-Reported Outcomes (PRO) Measures. The process included forward and back-translations, expert reconciliation, and cognitive debriefing.

**Results:** In 2011, the PPAQ was translated and tested on 20 patients with SVT (12 male; mean age 54.9 ± 17.4 years). Key challenges identified during the process included nuances in the clinical nomenclature of symptoms and the necessity of addressing gender-specific linguistic distinctions inherent to the Polish language. These were resolved through consultation with electrophysiology experts and by incorporating patient feedback during cognitive interviews.

**Conclusions:** The Polish version of the PPAQ demonstrated strong patient acceptance and established initial content validity. The linguistic and cultural hurdles encountered during this study offer a roadmap for researchers adapting similar patient-reported outcome measures into other gender-inflected languages.
